notes: Harbour Energy acquired Wintershall Dea upstream assets in September 2024. Maria was historically operated by Wintershall Dea Norge. Most public technical literature still references Wintershall.

MARIA.location: Halten Terrace, Norwegian Sea, ~20 km east of Kristin, ~45 km south of Heidrun, ~200 km off Kristiansund, PL 475 BS / PL 475 CS
MARIA.production_concept: Subsea tieback (two templates) to Equinor-operated Kristin platform for processing; water injection from Heidrun; oil export via Asgard C FSU and shuttle tanker; gas via Asgard Transport to Karsto
MARIA.drive_mechanism: Water injection for pressure support; wells equipped with gas lift

reservoir.formation: Garn Formation (primary); also Ile/Tilje/Not formations in stratigraphy
reservoir.structure: Well-defined horst block structure with moderate reservoir quality; low connectivity between geological layers (key issue reducing post-PDO reserves)

fluid_properties.api_gravity_estimate_basis: Garn Formation reservoirs on the Halten Terrace typically produce light, low-sulfur crude (Kristin condensate ~46 API; Tyrihans 40-42 API; Heidrun ~29 API from Are Fm). Maria is described as producing oil + associated gas with reservoir at 3800m depth, consistent with light crude/condensate (35-40 API). NOT publicly disclosed.
fluid_properties.gor_basis: Calculated from Sodir original in-place: 6.16 Gsm3 associated gas / 41.30 Msm3 oil = 149 Sm3/Sm3 (~840 scf/bbl). Indicates a saturated/near-saturated light oil with substantial gas cap.
fluid_properties.pressure_temperature_note: Not publicly disclosed for Maria. Adjacent Kristin field (deeper, ~5000m) is HPHT at 900 bar/180C. Maria at 3800m would be lower - estimated 400-500 bar initial pressure and 130-150 C based on regional Halten Terrace gradient.

volumetrics.recovery_factor_basis: 14.55 / 41.30 = 35.2% recoverable oil RF as of 2025. Original PDO estimated ~180 mmboe recoverable (~28.6 Msm3 oe), revised down significantly post-2018 due to poor inter-layer connectivity (per GeoExpro)

phase_2.purpose: Drain central part of Garn Formation previously not accessed

sources_consulted: Harbour Energy corporate website (what-we-do/norway, capital markets update Mar 2025, full-year results 2025)
GeoExpro (Maria still shut-in article on connectivity issues)
Offshore Technology / NS Energy Business / Offshore Energy industry coverage
ScienceDirect: Garn Formation in Maria and Lavrans fields (paywalled, not retrievable)
data_gaps: API gravity at reservoir - not publicly disclosed; Maria stream is co-processed with Kristin/Tyrihans so no separate blend assay exists
Initial reservoir pressure and temperature - not in public domain
Specific reservoir studies/SPE papers - the Garn Formation Maria sedimentology paper exists but is paywalled
